rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on attraction-effect/decoy choice processes, and the cited paper specifically tests mechanism accounts (attribute-weight/similarity vs value-shift) that the citing paper says it is extending beyond with an emotion-based fMRI mechanism. That makes the citation plausibly a direct theory/mechanism prior rather than an incidental mention. Even though the raw evidence is only a DOI match, the topical and construct overlap is strong enough to justify second-stage review.
